mechanism_narrative: If FEDFUNDS stays elevated relative to recent inflation, markets infer restrictive policy will persist and the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days as term premia and expected policy rates reprice upward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0 and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation is not falling for 2 consecutive monthly prints
failure_condition: Reject if, after the trigger, the 10-year yield falls in more than 60% of 20-trading-day windows or the average 20-day yield change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates labor market deterioration, pushes recession probability higher, and drives safe-haven demand for Treasuries; the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days when unemployment acceleration is present.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS declines month over month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by a positive 20-day yield change in more than 55% of cases or if yield changes are not statistically lower than unconditional averages.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in HOUST signals collapsing housing demand and weaker real activity expectations, reducing expected growth and inflation; the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 5 trading days as investors buy duration.
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of the 5-day yield change is positive in a majority of months when HOUST trends downward for at least 2 consecutive releases.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO improves while RSAFS also rises, the combination signals broader capacity utilization and stronger real demand, which lifts inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both rise on their latest monthly readings versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if joint up-moves in INDPRO and RSAFS are followed by lower 20-day yields in more than 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in RSAFS indicates weakening retail demand and a lower inflation path, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days as the market prices slower nominal growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls for 2 consecutive monthly releases or drops by more than 1% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales declines are followed by rising 20-day Treasury yields more than half the time or if the average post-event yield change is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-tightening pause, a flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate while CPIAUCSL remains sticky keeps real rates elevated but reduces additional policy-shock risk; the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 60 trading days as the market shifts from recession pricing toward term-premium normalization.
failure_condition: Reject if, during flat-rate/high-inflation periods, the 60-day yield change is negative in a majority of samples or if the sign is unstable across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS contracts and UNRATE rises together, the labor market is clearly deteriorating and recession fears dominate; the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 5 trading days as investors seek duration and cut growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month over month and UNRATE rises month over month in the same release window
failure_condition: Reject if the yield rises after the joint labor deterioration signal in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing rebound regime, rising INDPRO together with stronger PAYEMS indicates real activity is broadening, which lifts inflation and rate expectations; the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly prints while PAYEMS is flat or increasing
failure_condition: Reject if the combined recovery signal is followed by lower 20-day yields in more than 60% of cases or if effects vanish after controlling for baseline rate levels.
